decisions.rationale: Migration approval composed from accepted operational-anchor reviews: m86-2015-07-13-off-board-fare, m86-2015-07-13-real-time-information.
Migration approval composed from accepted operational-anchor reviews: ace-2025-09-15-bx20, ace-2025-09-15-bx3, ace-2025-09-15-bx7, ace-2025-09-15-q6.
Migration approval composed from accepted operational-anchor reviews: m15-2010-10-10-off-board-fare.

The official MTA Q1 row binds one realized onset and route to a three-member service-change package: a western extension, stop removals, and replacement of the prior Springfield Boulevard segment by Q36 service. Canonical members retain their literal service_pattern or bus_stop_or_boarding families. The official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context separately supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. Q36 is replacement context inside one treatment and is not an occurrence route.

The official MTA Q110 row binds a realized June 29, 2025 onset and the Q110 route to a three-member package in one evidence block: reroute/extension, discontinuation and Q82 replacement of the Hempstead Avenue segment, and stop removals. Canonical members retain their literal route_redesign, service_pattern, and bus_stop_or_boarding families; official project context supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. Q36 and Q82 remain treatment context and are never occurrence routes.

The official MTA Q19 row binds a realized August 31, 2025 onset, the Q19 route, and one canonical stop-removal treatment in the same evidence block. Q19 retained its existing routing, so this occurrence preserves the literal bus_stop_or_boarding family and does not invent a broader atomic route_redesign treatment or inherit any other Queens route scope.

The official MTA Q7 row binds one realized onset and route to a three-member routing package: a western reroute, discontinuation and replacement of the Sutter/Pitkin segment, and eastern shortening with replacement JFK Cargo service. Canonical members retain the source-level service_pattern family. The official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context separately supports route_redesign as the reviewed bundle analysis family. This decision does not atomize members, inherit scope from another route, or treat the aggregate bundle-container record as an atomic treatment.
